DISCOVERY, 302PLAINTIFF PEDRO RODRIGUEZ MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED TRUTH OF FACTS AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS OFF CALENDAR; MOTION WITHDRAWN PER REQUEST OF MOVING PARTY. EMAIL DATED 9/23/20. (302/MG)

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT LI ZHU, LLZ CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, AS TO THE DEFAULTS ENTERED ON JANUARY 21, 2020 AND JANUARY 23, 2020 IS GRANTED. "[T]HE PROVISIONS OF SECTION 473 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE ARE TO BE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED AND SOUND POLICY FAVORS THE DETERMINATION OF ACTIONS ON THEIR MERITS." (ZAMORA V. CLAYBORN CONTRACTING GROUP, INC. (2002) 28 CAL.4TH 249, 256.) IN THIS CASE, DEFENDANTS ESTABLISH THAT THE DEFAULTS WERE PROXIMATELY CAUSED BY ATTORNEY CHUNG'S ERROR. (CHUNG DEC., PAR. 5; MILTON V. PERCEPTUAL DEVELOP. CORP. (1997) 53 CAL.APP.4TH 861, 867 [ATTORNEY MUST BE PROXIMATE CAUSE FOR MANDATORY RELIEF].) CHUNG EXPLAINS THAT HE WAS ADVISING DEFENDANTS REGARDING THE CASE BUT FAILED TO FOLLOW THROUGH AND TELL DEFENDANTS TO TIMELY RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B), DEFENDANTS SHALL PAY $2,500 TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AS THE REASONABLE COST FOR OBTAINING THE DEFAULTS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO AMEND Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-10-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jann Noddin, Esq. (j_noddin@yahoo.com) and Patrick Romero Guillory, Esq. (415-285-1882; patrick@doloresparklaw.com) of Dolores Park Law PC for Plaintiff.
Gustavo Pena, Esq. (415-293-7071; Gustavo.Pena@LibertyMutual.com) of Law Offices of Santana & Vierra for Defendants, Chung.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PEDRO RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION TO AMEND Complaint:

Plaintiff's motion for leave to amend the complaint to seek punitive damages against Defendants is granted. Defendants Li Zhu and LLZ Corporation do not oppose the motion. Defendants Howard Chung and Chung Enterprises' untimely opposition raises disputed factual issues that are not properly considered on a motion for leave to amend. The proposed first amended complaint allege that plaintiff contracted a life-threatening disease as a result of ingesting E. coli bacterium at Defendants' restaurant caused by their repeated health code violations including illegal food handling practices. In light of the strong policy of liberality in amendment of pleadings, those allegations are sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages on the ground that Defendants operated their restaurant with a knowing disregard of the rights or safety of others. Whether Plaintiff's evidence is sufficient to meet the clear and convincing standard of proof of Civil Code section 3294 presents a question of fact for the trier of fact. Plaintiff's references to Code of Civil Procedure section 425.14 are misplaced, as that statute governs claims for punitive damages against religious corporations.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-20-2020 Continued To Jul-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT LI ZHU, LLZ CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, as to the defaults entered on January 21, 2020 and January 23, 2020 is granted. "[T]he provisions of section 473 of the Code of Civil Procedure are to be liberally construed and sound policy favors the determination of actions on their merits." (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 256.) In this case, defendants establish that the defaults were proximately caused by attorney Chung's error. (Chung Dec., par. 5; Milton v. Perceptual Develop. Corp. (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 861, 867 [attorney must be proximate cause for mandatory relief].) Chung explains that he was advising defendants regarding the case but failed to follow through and tell defendants to timely respond to the complaint. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), defendants shall pay $2,500 to plaintiff's counsel as the reasonable cost for obtaining the defaults. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceedings not reported.
